methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
ADASI, a sovereign-funded defense and security company at the EDGE stage, operates within a fragmented comp universe of 10 identifiable peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries. However, the narrow peer-matching approach does not yield sufficient datapoints for reliable triangulation.

The Peer-Median method is inapplicable here. While 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security broadly, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three comparable datapoints—the minimum threshold for statistical validity. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given ADASI's specialized sovereign-funding model and stage positioning, which are not representative of the broader Defense & Security market.

Without sufficient narrow comparables, traditional valuation triangulation cannot be responsibly executed. The analysis suggests that ADASI's valuation would be better informed through alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis (if revenue and growth trajectory are available), sovereign-funding benchmarks specific to EDGE Group entities, or transaction multiples from recent M&A in the defense tech subsector. Key uncertainties include the lack of public trading data for true peer comparison and the absence of revenue or margin data to anchor relative multiples. A more robust assessment requires either expanded comparable set refinement or introduction of fundamental operational metrics.
